title: Evidence Brief for Malaria Burden and Control Programs in Ethiopia: A Subnational Level Review
authors: National Data Management Center for Health (NDMC)
abstract: This evidence brief provides a detailed analysis of malaria incidence, prevalence, and control strategies across the regional states of Ethiopia. It highlights the role of community health extension workers in executing local insecticide-treated net distributions and indoor residual spraying, with focus on stakeholder collaboration in high-risk zones.

title: Ethiopia National Malaria Indicator Survey (EMIS) 2023: Community-based Intervention Coverage
abstract: A nationwide survey evaluating the ownership and utilization of long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs) and coverage of indoor residual spraying. The report details regional variations, stakeholder mapping of local NGOs executing malaria prevention campaigns, and efficacy of district-level community mobilization.

title: Community-based Malaria Case Management Guidelines for Health Extension Workers in Ethiopia
abstract: An operational manual detailing standard procedures for malaria rapid diagnostic testing (RDT) and artemether-lumefantrine treatment at the health post level. It provides the framework for community-based health extension packages in rural districts.
